spec.expr: (sum by(host) (netdata_disk_space_GiB_average{job="netdata_linux", dimension="used"})) / (sum by(host) (netdata_disk_space_GiB_average{job="netdata_linux", dimension="used"}) + sum by(host) (netdata_disk_space_GiB_average{job="netdata_linux", dimension="avail"})) * 100

spec.description: Fleet-wide average of the per-host anomaly rate. Netdata smooths this over 5 min (default `anomaly detection grouping duration`). <1% healthy, 1–2% watch, >2% sustained = systemic event.
spec.title: Global Anomaly Rate

spec.description: Absolute count of dimensions currently flagged anomalous across the selected fleet. Compare against Total Dimensions in Tab 6 — anomaly rate is this ÷ total. Sudden jumps usually mean a deploy or config change.
spec.title: Anomalous Dimensions (Now)

spec.description: Per-host anomaly rate over time. Patterns to spot: one line diverging = isolated host issue. All lines rising together at the same instant = fleet-wide event (deploy, network, upstream). Slow drift up over hours = capacity / saturation creeping in.
spec.title: Fleet Anomaly Rate Trend

spec.description: Time-series view of services matching the $context filter, one line per (context, host). With $context='All' this is noisy — set $context to a specific service (e.g. `mysql.queries`) or regex (`mysql\..*`) to drill in. Look for sustained elevation vs sharp transient spikes.
spec.title: Selected Context Trend (filter via $context)

spec.description: Time-series of every subsystem prefix found in your contexts (one line per first-dot-segment). Shows the relative weight of each bucket and how it moves over time. Subsystems that rise together usually share a root cause.
spec.title: All Subsystems — Trend

spec.description: Sum of anomaly rates per subsystem, counting only contexts currently >1%. Vertical alignment of multiple subsystem peaks at the same time = correlated multi-subsystem event. Use this with Tab 1's heatmap to confirm scope.
spec.title: Simultaneous Subsystem Pressure

spec.description: Hosts whose anomaly rate is currently above their detector threshold (default 1%). If this is low but Global Rate is high → one or two hosts dominate; check Top Services table or Tab 2.
spec.title: Hosts In Anomaly State

spec.expr: (sum by(instance, mount_point) (netdata_disk_space_GiB_average{dimension="used", instance=~"$instance"}) / sum by(instance, mount_point) (netdata_disk_space_GiB_average{instance=~"$instance"})) * 100 > 90
